title: How can I find the Board that links to the Form that I have access to?
excerpt: I have access to a Form (submission view) but cannot find the board that the form relates to or to which the results are going to. Please can anyone help advise me? Thanks!

posts.author: alrobjack (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: I have access to a Form (submission view) but cannot find the board that the form relates to or to which the results are going to. Please can anyone help advise me? Thanks!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Alex! Shannon here with the monday team. I’d like to share a shortcut with you!

Right-click on your form, and from the dropdown menu, select “View Page Source.” From there, enter Ctrl + F (or Command + F, if you’re on a Mac device) to search for “board.” You are looking for “board_id”:

Screenshot 2025-09-02 at 10.42.48 AM385×164 12.4 KB

From there, you’ll enter your board ID after your account’s URL to pull up the form’s board. For example, this is my demo form’s board URL: monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done.

Enter the board ID after “/boards/” in the URL. I hope this helps!

posts.author: alrobjack (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Shannon,

Thanks for your response, much appreciated.

Apologies, I should perhaps have been clearer in my request… By Form I meant that I have a link to a form - there is no form icon to right click on, so I cannot do so to then select “View Page Source”…

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Alex! No problem at all. Perhaps I could have been a bit more clear!

The shortcut I’ve shared does not require you to utilize any icons. You just need the link to the form. With the form open, right-click directly on the page, like so:

Screenshot 2025-09-03 at 9.58.41 AM700×813 62.1 KB

A new tab will open in your web browser window, where you can search for “board” to find the “board_id.”

posts.author: alrobjack (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Shannon,

This is great - thank you very much for clarifying. This makes perfect sense now and has really helped.

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Of course, Alex. Happy to help!
